candidate: sm120 transpose-free K-GEMM conv router (no cuDNN/nchwToNhwc); fused epilogue reused from sm75
mechanism: causal conv = K shifted (B,S,H)@(H,E) matmuls, x in native layout; kills copy_ 48% + ~870us transposes
correctness: by-construction conv-as-GEMM; bench grad parity is the gate

why: launch+copy explosion: 161 launches/iter, 240 aten::mm (42% CUDA), 760 aten::copy_ (31%, from sliced-noncontig matmul inputs + per-k .to() casts + .float()), 180 add_; total 7.42ms CUDA vs cudnn 5.57 vs compiled 4.84
lesson: multi-torch-op conv decomposition is launch/copy-bound on Blackwell too (matches T4 cuBLAS-K-GEMM 0.35x). Only a SINGLE fused Triton kernel can win.
next: candidate #2 = fused Triton conv (one launch, read-once, in-register epilogue)

candidate: single-launch fused Triton conv router (FusedConvRouterFused): fwd conv+sigmoid+topk+gather in one kernel; grad_x fused kernel (H-tiled); grad_W fused reduction kernel
mechanism: ~4 launches vs cuDNN 75, x read once, zero layout transposes/copies, bf16 tl.dot fp32-accum
note: 3 blind hand-written kernels; first run = compile+correctness(idx/grad/count)+perf gate; expect possible correctness iteration

profile: _conv_router_dw_kernel 58% CUDA (2.9ms,145us/call); dx 4.9% (12us); fwd 21.7% (54us); ~47 launches vs eager 54
diagnosis: (1) grad/count fail = tl.dot defaults to TF32 on fp32 inputs -> ~1e-3 logit perturbation flips near-tie top-k -> a few tokens reroute -> large local grad delta + count mismatch (idx-agree rounds to 1.0). (2) dw is bwd bottleneck = grid only K*(H/64)=32 programs each serial-reducing all N=16384 -> under-occupied on 188 SMs.
next: candidate #3 (this push): input_precision=ieee on all tl.dot (fixes correctness gate, no-op on bf16 timing) + split-K over N in dw (32->512 programs, atomic-add fp32 accum)

profile: our CUDA 2.60ms vs eager 4.86ms (1.87x less work); dw 145->23us/call (split-K 32->512 programs), dx 12us, fwd 54us (now the largest single GPU cost); ~51 launches vs eager 54
confirms: TF32 diagnosis correct (ieee fixed count+grad at zero bf16-timing cost); split-K fixed bwd occupancy
remaining_headroom: forward kernel 54us @ 1.18x = the only soft spot left; skinny (M=32,N=16,K=512) matmul + in-register top-k epilogue, 512 programs, BLOCK_S pinned 32 by fp32-check SMEM. Optional next candidate.

candidate: sm120 MLP router HYBRID: cuBLAS GEMM fwd + fused-Triton (K=1 conv dx/split-K-dw) bwd
prior: all-cuBLAS FusedMLPRouter (user count-fuse + drop-.float) = fwd+bwd 1.09x (fwd 1.33x WIN, bwd 0.58x LOSS), grad PASS
diagnosis: bwd launch-bound: same 2 cuBLAS mm's as eager (aten::mm 775us both sides) + un-fused cast/norm glue eager compiles away -> 49 launches vs eager 30. A linear router IS a K=1 conv (no shift), so grad_x=gl@W and grad_w=gl.T@x are exactly the conv dx/split-K-dw kernels that won 1.62x.
change: keep cuBLAS fwd (1.33x > fused-conv 1.18x); replace the 2 cuBLAS bwd mm's + cast with _conv_router_dx_kernel + split-K _conv_router_dw_kernel at K=1 (W as (E,H,1))
correctness: dx/dw proven on the K=4 fp32 gate (rel 2.4e-4); K=1 is a strict subset (shift=0, no causal mask)

candidate: fuse norm_topk_prob + routed_scaling into one Triton Function (_TopkNormalize) — wired into BOTH fused_router (conv) and mlp_router wrappers
diagnosis: the lean MLP baseline exposed the un-fused eager norm tail (div 4/iter + mul 5 + sum 3 + fill 5 ~= 17 launches the compiled baseline fuses) as the dominant launch tax keeping the CPU-bound combined step from winning; the hybrid already fixed the bwd kernels (isolated bwd 0.58x->0.75x, pure CUDA 1.71->1.38ms) but combined stayed launch-bound
change: fwd kernel: O=SCALE*w/(sum_k w+eps); bwd kernel: sum-to-1 Jacobian grad_w=SCALE/s*(go-<go,p>), p=w/s. ~17 launches -> 2. sm120-only (sm75 reference untouched).
scope: also applied to the conv champion wrapper (free launch cut there too) — re-validate the conv `router` bench stays PASS and >=1.45x
expect: mlp combined break-even -> win; conv 1.45x -> higher

why: _TopkNormalize fused the eager norm+scale tail (~17 launches) into 2 kernels; backward 1.62->2.30x

result: SHIP AS FWD-WIN (bwd cannot beat eager)
verdict: NO STRUCTURAL SEAM in the bwd: grad_x=gl@W and grad_w=gl.T@x are 2 clean GEMMs cuBLAS does optimally + eager fuses the sigmoid-bwd/scatter/norm glue into ~3 launches. Launch-bound tiny op; hybrid (conv-K=1 kernels) bwd 0.57x == all-cuBLAS bwd 0.58x -> the Triton kernels did NOT help (nothing to fix). K=1 conv == linear mathematically (grad rel 4e-7 proves it) but the conv kernels were built to dodge cuDNN's layout tax, which the linear doesn't have.
recurring_lesson: beat compile only at structural seams; the conv router HAS one (cuDNN transposes), the linear backward does NOT.
reco: revert sm120 hybrid -> plain cuBLAS FusedMLPRouter (equal speed, simpler, arch-agnostic), keep fused norm
